game_name: Wizards & Warriors III: Kuros: Visions of Power

danger_signals.visual_form: Malkil's magical attacks and dark magic bolt visuals
Horizontal/vertical scrolling boss levels at guild test completion

danger_signals.meaning: Major threat for obtaining final gem; high-difficulty encounter

danger_signals.visual_form: Doors and windows inaccessible without proper disguise
danger_signals.meaning: Areas dangerous or forbidden without proper character class ability

safety_signals.visual_form: Guild buildings for each character class (knight, thief, wizard)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression points; ability upgrade centers
Safe rest/commerce areas; health restoration and item purchase

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Malkil as spirit; magical attacks distinct from physical combat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Throne room control; city of Piedup under occupation
Castle throne room; city of Piedup as legitimate seat; princess locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kuros in knight disguise with bronze/silver/gold variants
Kuros in thief disguise with bronze/silver/gold variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Thief guild; window/door access; covert movement

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Kuros in wizard disguise with bronze/silver/gold variants

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Malkil's spirit escape after IceFire Mountain defeat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Magic bolt striking Kuros; armor/memory/honor removal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kuros weakened; months of wilderness wandering; arrival at Piedup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle/throne room under dark wizard control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Good King James imprisoned/deposed; city under tyranny; restricted access to palace

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three separate realms available (town, underworld, palace)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Open-ended exploration; statue collection for guild tests; progressive ability gains
New areas become accessible; improved weapons, magic, and stealth abilities unlock

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four secret gems; three princess locations; dragon in underworld
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hidden passage to throne room unlocked only after gem acquisition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Freeing King James; defeating Malkil in throne room
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final confrontation; restoration of kingdom order

environmental_storytelling.example: Kuros assumes knight, thief, and wizard disguises to 'travel through town undetected' — visual disguise communicates his hidden status and adaptive survival strategy in occupied Piedup
Three realms (town, underworld, palace) accessible simultaneously communicate layered political geography: civilian space, hidden depths, and seat of power
Separate guild locations for knight/thief/wizard classes communicate organized power structures and class-based society

environmental_storytelling.technique: Statue quest as political legitimacy tokens
environmental_storytelling.example: Bronze/silver/gold statue progression toward guilds represents earning credentials and proving worthiness to aid in restoration
Three hidden princesses in occupied city communicate covert royal lineage preservation; gem rewards signal legitimacy succession chain
Purchasable keys, food, drink, and information at inns communicate Kuros's transition from wilderness exile to functioning city agent

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Flees to Piedup; strikes Kuros with magic bolt; seizes throne; awaits confrontation in throne room
Wanders wilderness; arrives at Piedup; assumes disguises; explores nonlinearly; collects statues; rescues princesses; builds strength
Held captive in palace; awaits rescue; legitimacy restored upon freedom
Remain in concealment; grant gems upon rescue; signal marriage/alliance to Kuros
Conduct tests for class advancement; grant disguises and abilities upon success
Defends final gem; serves as significant combat encounter

notes: Wizards & Warriors III communicates political collapse and restoration through visual transformation systems (disguises), spatial geography (three realms), and quest progression (statue→guild→ability→access). The game's nonlinear structure mirrors Kuros's fragmented identity recovery. Malkil's spirit form is a visual manifestation of supernatural threat persisting beyond physical defeat. Guild systems communicate class-based social order; princess rescue mechanics signal restoration of legitimate succession. The game heavily relies on visual disguise as narrative tool — appearance directly determines world-state access, making cosmetic changes functionally political statements.
